subject: Bluetooth mouse keeps disconnecting
description: Wireless mouse drops connection several times per day; works again after toggle or reboot.

ts_steps.title: Battery and physical range
ts_steps.action: Replace or recharge batteries; keep mouse within 1–2 m of Mac with clear line of sight.

ts_steps.title: Reduce RF interference
ts_steps.action: Move bus-powered Thunderbolt/USB4 hubs and fast USB-C accessories away from the Mac and mouse; metal desk clutter can attenuate 2.4 GHz.

ts_steps.title: Forget device
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Bluetooth → (i) on mouse → Forget This Device → confirm.

ts_steps.title: Pair again
ts_steps.action: Put mouse in pairing mode per vendor. Pair from Bluetooth list. Confirm Connected and battery % if shown.

ts_steps.title: Bluetooth toggle
ts_steps.action: System Settings → Bluetooth → Off, wait 15 s, On. Disconnect other Bluetooth audio devices that might contend for the radio.

ts_steps.title: Quit conflicting apps
ts_steps.action: Quit remote desktop or input-capture apps that may reset HID focus. Cmd+Q Safari tabs with heavy WebRTC rarely matter but close meeting apps using the mouse exclusively.

ts_steps.title: Restart macOS
ts_steps.action: Apple menu → Restart — clears stuck Bluetooth daemon state.

ts_steps.title: Safe Mode test
ts_steps.action: Shut down, power on holding power until startup options → volume → hold Shift → Continue in Safe Mode. Test mouse stability briefly.

ts_steps.title: Login items
ts_steps.action: If stable only in Safe Mode: System Settings → General → Login Items — disable third-party agents in batches and reboot between tests.

ts_steps.title: Hardware swap
ts_steps.action: Test same mouse on another Apple Silicon Mac or swap mouse — isolates device vs environment.

internal_information: Apple Silicon integrated Bluetooth; RF and USB4 noise dominate. No Intel/SMC narrative.
